rubric_identity.one_sentence_purpose: Evaluate CVs for senior IC or light player-coach roles that ship production AI platforms and features, prove architecture and reliability outcomes, and translate cross-functional needs into scalable applied AI systems.
rubric_identity.core_persona: Staff or principal-level IC who architects production AI platforms, delivers evaluation and reliability outcomes, and provides technical mentorship without formal people management.
job_overlay_notes: Player-coach scope appears in 15/34 roles; adjust mentoring emphasis based on JD signals for team-facing responsibility.
Evaluation and quality measurement is a differentiator at 44.1%; weight higher when JD emphasizes production quality, benchmarking, or AI safety.
RAG, agents, and prompt engineering each appear in fewer than 30% of roles; require JD evidence before weighting these as primary signals.
Domain distribution is shallow (fintech 3/34, healthcare 3/34); avoid domain specialist framing unless JD explicitly requires it.

what_good_looks_like: Uses 'Staff AI Engineer' or 'Principal AI Engineer' headline aligned with title families (staff_engineer 20/34, principal_engineer 13/34).
Includes 'platform', 'architecture', or 'system design' 2-3 times given 100% platform design signal.
Mentions 'reliability', 'evaluation', 'scalability' as strongest keywords from valued evidence types.
Incorporates 'collaboration' and 'communication' as soft-skill keywords reflecting 85.3% and 67.6% frequency.
Avoids keyword stuffing with frameworks that individually appear below 15% in the sample.
common_failures: Uses 'Technical Architect' or 'Engineering Manager' headlines that miss category title families.
Stacks GenAI buzzwords (LLMs, agents, RAG, guardrails, vector DBs) without outcome evidence.
Omits platform and architecture language despite 100% signal strength.
Over-indexes on niche tools like Grafana, Prometheus, or specific cloud providers without JD match.

what_to_adjust_per_jd: If JD emphasizes RAG or retrieval, include 'RAG', 'retrieval', 'hybrid search' 1-2 times.
If JD mentions agents or orchestration, add 'workflow orchestration' or 'tool-calling' once.
If JD lists specific frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex), mention only if candidate has true evidence.
If JD emphasizes evaluation or quality, prioritize MRR, NDCG, benchmarking language.
company_domain_overrides: Healthcare JDs: only add compliance language if explicitly required and candidate has evidence.
Enterprise SaaS JDs: emphasize 'multi-tenant', 'scalable platform', 'cross-team adoption'.
region_overrides: EEA roles: no native language requirements evidenced; English proficiency sufficient.
Remote-first signals common; frame distributed collaboration as working style.

9_10: Headline precisely matches Staff/Principal AI Engineer title family with platform qualifier.
Keywords cover platform design, reliability, evaluation, collaboration with natural density.
Technical terms align with JD stack without unsupported framework claims.
ATS-friendly formatting with clear section headers and consistent structure.
7_8: Headline is adjacent (e.g., 'Staff Software Engineer - AI') but recognizable.
Core keywords present but slightly under- or over-indexed relative to JD.
Most technical terms supported by evidence; minor gaps in keyword coverage.
5_6: Headline misaligned (e.g., 'Technical Architect') requiring mental mapping by reviewer.
Some keyword stuffing without outcome evidence.
0_4: Headline uses manager/director titles unsupported by evidence or category pattern.
Critical keywords absent; CV would not surface in ATS keyword screening.
Heavy buzzword loading with no evidence backing.

red_flags: Headline claims 'Director of AI' or 'Head of AI' without management evidence.
Keyword density exceeds natural flow, suggesting optimization gaming.
Framework mentions exceed candidate's evidenced usage.
Missing 'platform' or 'architecture' language despite category's 100% signal.

what_good_looks_like: Achievement bullets follow ARIS/STAR format with quantified outcomes (users, throughput, latency, uptime).
Platform architecture achievements prove multi-team reuse and adoption scale.
Evaluation and quality achievements include measurable lift (accuracy, MRR, NDCG, incident reduction).
Reliability achievements cite SLO attainment, uptime percentages, or incident reduction rates.
Mentoring achievements quantify engineers mentored and career progression outcomes.
common_failures: Bullets describe activities without outcomes ('Worked on AI platform').
Missing quantification for scale, latency, reliability, or adoption metrics.
Evaluation work described without quality metrics (precision, recall, MRR, NDCG).
Leadership framed as responsibility rather than measurable team-level impact.

what_to_adjust_per_jd: If JD emphasizes scale, prioritize throughput and user volume metrics.
If JD emphasizes reliability, surface uptime, incident reduction, SLO metrics first.
If JD emphasizes cost optimization, include inference cost or efficiency metrics if evidenced.
If JD emphasizes quality, foreground evaluation metrics and quality improvement percentages.
company_domain_overrides: Startup JDs: emphasize speed-to-production and iteration velocity metrics.
Enterprise JDs: emphasize adoption scale, multi-team reuse, and reliability metrics.
Platform companies: emphasize API throughput, integration count, and developer adoption.
region_overrides: EEA data privacy context: if GDPR compliance is relevant, mention governance outcomes only if evidenced.

9_10: Every priority bullet has specific, verifiable metrics tied to business or technical outcomes.
Achievement archetypes match category pattern: platform adoption, evaluation quality, reliability, scale.
Metrics span the category's valued evidence types: architecture, delivery, reliability, scale, cost.
Cross-functional impact is quantified (teams onboarded, stakeholder alignment outcomes).
7_8: Most bullets have metrics; 1-2 bullets rely on directional claims without specifics.
Achievement archetypes largely align with category but minor gaps in coverage.
Reliability and scale metrics present but latency or cost metrics underrepresented.
5_6: Metrics present in fewer than half of priority bullets.
Achievements describe scope without proving impact.
Category-critical signals (platform, evaluation, reliability) mentioned but not quantified.
0_4: Bullets are activity-focused with no measurable outcomes.
Missing evidence for platform delivery, evaluation, or reliability impact.

red_flags: Metrics appear fabricated or suspiciously round (e.g., 'reduced latency by 90%' without context).
Scale claims exceed plausible scope for role or company.
Evaluation metrics claimed without methodology evidence.
Leadership impact claimed without quantified team outcomes.

what_good_looks_like: CV demonstrates platform design and architecture scope matching category's 100% signal.
Senior IC or player-coach framing aligns with role scope (IC 19/34, player-coach 15/34).
Applied AI patterns (evaluation, RAG, agents, guardrails) appear where JD requires them.
Reliability and production operation experience matches category pain points.
Cross-functional collaboration evidence supports category's 85.3% soft-skill signal.
common_failures: CV emphasizes research or academic work when category signals 2.9% research-heavy.
People management framing when hiring appears in only 8.8% of roles.
Narrow tool or domain specialization unsupported by JD requirements.
GenAI features dominate narrative without platform/architecture grounding.

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Match JD's specific AI stack requirements only when candidate has true evidence.
Adjust player-coach vs pure IC framing based on JD team-facing signals.
Weight evaluation/quality language based on JD emphasis on AI safety or benchmarking.
Include distributed systems or microservices depth if JD explicitly requires it.
company_domain_overrides: Fintech domain: emphasize latency, throughput, reliability if JD mentions trading or real-time.
Healthcare domain: include compliance only if JD requires and candidate has evidence.
Consulting domain: avoid unless repeated work history proves consulting background.
Enterprise SaaS: emphasize platform reuse, multi-tenant architecture, enterprise scale.
region_overrides: EEA roles frequently show remote-first signals; present distributed collaboration as strength.
No native language requirements evidenced; do not claim language skills beyond what's true.

9_10: CV addresses all JD must-haves with specific, evidenced claims.
Role scope (IC vs player-coach) precisely matches JD signals.
Technical stack alignment is verifiable and non-inflated.
Soft skills (collaboration, communication, mentoring) surface appropriately for JD emphasis.
7_8: Most JD must-haves covered; 1-2 minor gaps addressable in interview.
Role scope mostly aligned with minor framing adjustments needed.
Technical stack has minor gaps but core competencies match.
5_6: JD alignment is partial; multiple must-haves weakly covered or missing.
Role scope misalignment (e.g., manager framing for IC role).
Technical stack gaps in category-critical areas (platform, evaluation, reliability).
0_4: CV does not address JD must-haves.
Fundamental persona mismatch (research-focused for applied role, manager for IC role).
Technical evidence absent for JD-required competencies.

red_flags: Claims domain expertise (healthcare, fintech, consulting) without repeated work history.
Overstates management scope relative to category's modest management signals.
Claims niche tool ownership (Grafana, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry) without explicit evidence.
Research or PhD framing when JD is applied-AI focused.

what_good_looks_like: Demonstrates senior IC authority through architecture decisions and technical judgment.
Shows cross-team influence via design reviews, mentoring, and stakeholder alignment.
Communicates technical trade-offs to non-technical stakeholders without overstating org scope.
Mentoring framed as technical uplift (code quality, architecture patterns) not line management.
Player-coach evidence supports light leadership without formal management claims.
common_failures: Claims formal org ownership, hiring leadership, or performance management without evidence.
Uses manager/director/VP language when evidence supports IC scope.
Overstates strategic influence beyond technical architecture decisions.
Describes leadership abstractly without concrete technical influence outcomes.

what_to_adjust_per_jd: If JD mentions tech lead responsibilities, emphasize architecture influence and code review.
If JD mentions player-coach, include mentoring outcomes with career progression evidence.
If JD emphasizes cross-functional work, surface stakeholder alignment and communication.
Avoid management framing entirely if JD is pure IC focused.
company_domain_overrides: Startup context: frame influence through shipping velocity and architectural ownership.
Enterprise context: frame influence through cross-team standards and platform adoption.
Platform company: frame influence through API design and developer experience decisions.
region_overrides: No region-specific executive presence adjustments required for EEA roles.

9_10: Technical authority evident through architecture ownership and design influence across teams.
Mentoring outcomes quantified with engineer growth and promotion evidence.
Cross-functional influence demonstrated without overstating management scope.
Communication with stakeholders shows translation between technical and business contexts.
7_8: Technical influence evident but cross-team scope somewhat limited.
Mentoring present but outcomes less quantified.
Stakeholder communication implied rather than explicitly demonstrated.
5_6: Leadership framing present but not differentiated from individual contributor scope.
0_4: Claims manager/director scope unsupported by evidence.
No technical influence or mentoring evidence.
Leadership language appears inflated relative to actual scope.

red_flags: Claims formal people management or performance review ownership without explicit evidence.
Uses 'built the team' or 'owned hiring' when hiring signal is only 8.8% in category.
Director/VP/Head of AI title when evidence supports staff/principal IC scope.
Strategic or organizational leadership claims beyond technical architecture scope.
Unsupported people-management framing for senior IC category.

what_good_looks_like: All metrics are verifiable and plausible given role scope and company context.
Titles match actual positions held; no title inflation from senior to staff/principal.
Technical claims supported by project evidence or work history.
Scope of responsibility matches realistic expectations for stated role level.
No claims for skills explicitly marked do_not_claim in evidence (fine-tuning, RLHF, custom embeddings).
common_failures: Fabricated or inflated metrics (e.g., '10x improvement' without methodology).
Title inflation from actual role (Senior Engineer claiming Staff/Principal).
Claims fine-tuning, RLHF, or custom embedding work without evidence.
PhD or publication claims not supported by actual credentials.
Scope inflation (individual project framed as company-wide platform).

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Verify any JD-specific technical claims (specific frameworks, tools) against evidence.
Check domain expertise claims against work history repetition.
Validate scale claims against company size and role scope.
company_domain_overrides: Startup claims: verify scale metrics are plausible for company stage.
Enterprise claims: verify cross-team influence matches org structure.
Consulting claims: avoid unless repeated consulting work history exists.
region_overrides: No region-specific anti-hallucination adjustments for EEA.

9_10: All claims traceable to specific evidence in work history or projects.
Metrics are specific, contextualized, and plausible.
Titles exactly match actual positions; no inflation.
Technical scope claims verified against documented experience.
No unsupported claims from unsafe_or_weak_framing list.
7_8: Most claims evidenced; 1-2 claims require clarification but not implausible.
Metrics generally credible with minor verification gaps.
Titles accurate; scope claims slightly broad but defensible.
5_6: Multiple claims lack clear evidence trail.
Some metrics appear rounded or generic.
Title or scope slightly inflated relative to evidence.
0_4: Significant fabrication suspected: metrics, titles, or technical claims unsupported.
Claims skills explicitly marked do_not_claim (fine-tuning, RLHF, custom embeddings).
PhD, publication, or research claims without credential evidence.
Scope dramatically exceeds plausible responsibility for role.

red_flags: Claims fine-tuning pipelines or custom embedding training without evidence.
Claims RLHF experience when explicitly marked do_not_claim.
PhD or publication claims not supported by education section.
Metrics lack context or methodology (e.g., 'reduced latency 90%' without baseline).
Title inflation: actual Senior Engineer claiming Staff/Principal scope.
Claims deep healthcare, fintech, or consulting expertise without repeated work history.
Claims observability tool ownership (Grafana, Prometheus, Loki, Tempo, OpenTelemetry) without explicit evidence.
Claims end-to-end compliance, HIPAA, or responsible AI program ownership without documentation.
Unsupported claims of hiring leadership when category signal is 8.8%.
Research or model invention claims from applied platform work.

pass_criteria: CV demonstrates platform design or architecture scope (table-stakes at 100%).
CV shows production AI delivery evidence, not just research or experimentation.
CV includes reliability, evaluation, or scalability outcomes.
CV reflects senior IC or player-coach scope, not pure manager.
fail_conditions: No platform or architecture evidence despite category's 100% signal.
Pure research focus when research-heavy appears in only 2.9% of roles.
No production delivery evidence; only experimentation or POC work.
Exclusively people-management framing without IC technical scope.

pass_criteria: No claims for skills explicitly marked do_not_claim (fine-tuning, RLHF, custom embeddings).
No formal management claims when evidence supports IC scope.
No domain specialist claims without repeated work history.
fail_conditions: Claims fine-tuning, RLHF, or custom embedding training without evidence.
Claims formal org ownership or hiring leadership when hiring signal is 8.8%.
Claims deep healthcare, fintech, observability, or consulting expertise without work history proof.
Claims Grafana, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, or Oracle Cloud ownership without explicit evidence.
Claims end-to-end compliance or responsible AI program ownership without documentation.

pass_criteria: CV presents as senior IC or player-coach, not formal manager or research lead.
Technical authority framed through architecture and delivery, not org ownership.
Applied AI positioning, not research-centric or academic framing.
Collaboration and communication evident without executive leadership inflation.
fail_conditions: CV presents as manager/director when category is senior IC focused.
Research or PhD-led positioning when research-heavy is 2.9% of category.
Executive leadership or C-suite framing for IC role.
Pure GenAI buzzword positioning without platform/architecture grounding.

scoring_guidance.how_to_interpret_executive_presence: For this senior IC category, executive_presence measures architectural authority, technical judgment, cross-team design influence, and mentoring impact WITHOUT formal management. Penalize any framing that implies direct reports, hiring ownership, performance management, or org-building beyond what evidence supports. Player-coach evidence (mentoring, technical guidance) is positive; manager/director framing is a red flag unless explicitly evidenced.
scoring_guidance.how_to_penalize_unsupported_claims: Apply a 2-point penalty per unsupported claim in anti_hallucination scoring. Claims for do_not_claim skills (fine-tuning, RLHF, custom embeddings) trigger automatic gate failure. Metrics without context or methodology receive 1-point penalty. Title inflation from evidenced role level receives 2-point penalty. Domain expertise claims without repeated work history receive 1.5-point penalty.
scoring_guidance.how_to_handle_category_vs_job_tradeoffs: Category provides baseline expectations (platform design 100%, evaluation 44%, reliability valued). When JD diverges, weight JD requirements higher but do not ignore category signals entirely. If JD requires niche skills below 15% category frequency, verify candidate evidence carefully before crediting. If JD omits category table-stakes (platform, reliability), still expect some evidence but weight JD-specific signals proportionally higher.

evidence_ledger.rubric_rule: Platform design is table-stakes at 100% category frequency; CVs must demonstrate architecture scope.
Title families concentrate in staff_engineer (20/34) and principal_engineer (13/34); headlines should align.
Evaluation/quality is strongest AI differentiator at 44.1%; should be weighted when JD emphasizes quality.
Management signals are limited (hiring 8.8%, performance management 2.9%); penalize formal management claims.
Fine-tuning, RLHF, and custom embedding training are explicitly do_not_claim; trigger gate failure if claimed.
Research-heavy roles appear in only 2.9% of sample; penalize research-centric or academic positioning.
Collaboration (85.3%) and communication (67.6%) are highest soft-skill signals; should surface in CV.
Mentoring appears in 58.8% of roles; frame as technical uplift, not line management.
